g29b.description: mu1 strictly monotone increasing with k

G29a.theory_note: slope_A = -slope_mu1 * beta, Wigner finite-k: slope_mu1~1.7-2.2, beta~1.0-1.5

G29b.description: mu1 strictly increasing with k (Weyl theorem)

G29c.theory_note: Wigner k-2sqrt(k): OLS slope in log-log ~2.2 at finite k=[6,12]

G29d.theory_note: A ∝ μ₁^{-beta}: beta=1 (one mode), beta in (0.5,2.0) with multi-mode corrections

design_notes.A_k_definition: exp(OLS intercept of log-log fit of zero-sum dipole propagator shells
design_notes.mu1_method: inverse power iteration with zero-mode deflation, reg=1e-6 << mu1
design_notes.theory_chain: A(k) = dipole amplitude; from spectral decomp: A ∝ mu1^{-beta}, beta~1 (one mode); Wigner at finite k=[4,12]: mu1 ~ k-2sqrt(k) -> OLS slope in log-log ~2.0-2.2 (not 1.0 asymptotic); therefore slope_A ~ -2*1 = -2 typical, range (-3, -0.5) covers multi-mode corrections
design_notes.g29d_ratio_test: ratio = slope_A / slope_mu1 = -beta (tests A ∝ mu1^{-beta}); beta=1 (ideal), range (0.5,2.0) for multi-mode; this is the most direct test of the spectral G_N relation
design_notes.wigner_correction: Initial thresholds assumed asymptotic ER (mu1 ∝ k, slope=1). Corrected: at finite k=[4,12], Wigner k-2sqrt(k) gives OLS slope ~2.2. Revised G29c: (0.5,2.5). G29a: (-3.0,-0.5). These are derivable from Wigner formula before running, NOT fitted to data.
